windows.objective: Loss-tolerant precursor: prove EDL, power, pilot ISRU, ECLSS testbed
enables: 640 kWe of storm-proof power — roughly 533 homes' worth, with 0 kW of headroom over the installed load.
Batteries hold 3.8 MWh — 6 hours of full-base power with zero generation (dust storms are a non-event on fission).
Fuel plant produces ~0.9 t of methalox per day — a full Starship return load every 4.3 years (pilot proof, not production).
322 t of propellant banked by the 2033-03 window — 0.2 Starship return flights' worth.
300 m³ of pressurized volume beyond the ships — long-duration habitable space for ~12 people.
Robot workforce: 10 humanoids and 2 rovers — the only crew on the planet.
EDL track record: landings so far demonstrate ~40% reliability (rule-of-three floor); this window's ships each land with ~50% odds.
251 t of hardware on the surface — 0.6× the mass of the ISS, and 0.03% of the ~1,000,000 t Musk estimates for a self-sufficient city.

windows.objective: Fuel factory: full rate-matched ISRU chain + the reactors it drags
enables: 1,760 kWe of storm-proof power — roughly 1,467 homes' worth, with 18 kW of headroom over the installed load.
Batteries hold 10.5 MWh — 6 hours of full-base power with zero generation (dust storms are a non-event on fission).
Fuel plant produces ~3.6 t of methalox per day — a full Starship return load every 13 months.
1,826 t of propellant banked by the 2035-05 window — 1.3 Starship return flights' worth.
300 m³ of pressurized volume beyond the ships — long-duration habitable space for ~12 people.
Robot workforce: 20 humanoids and 4 rovers — the only crew on the planet.
EDL track record: landings so far demonstrate ~70% reliability (rule-of-three floor); this window's ships each land with ~62% odds.
562 t of hardware on the surface — 1.3× the mass of the ISS, and 0.06% of the ~1,000,000 t Musk estimates for a self-sufficient city.

windows.objective: Second plant (2x production) + habitat cluster + deep caches
enables: 3,040 kWe of storm-proof power — roughly 2,533 homes' worth, with 43 kW of headroom over the installed load.
Batteries hold 18.0 MWh — 6 hours of full-base power with zero generation (dust storms are a non-event on fission).
Fuel plant produces ~6.3 t of methalox per day — a full Starship return load every 7 months.
4,941 t of propellant banked by the 2037-07 window — 3.5 Starship return flights' worth.
1,500 m³ of pressurized volume beyond the ships — long-duration habitable space for ~60 people.
90 t of cached food/water/O2 — a crew of 12 could live on it for 49 months with zero recycling.
Robot workforce: 50 humanoids and 8 rovers — the only crew on the planet.
EDL track record: landings so far demonstrate ~85% reliability (rule-of-three floor); this window's ships each land with ~74% odds.
1,123 t of hardware on the surface — 2.7× the mass of the ISS, and 0.11% of the ~1,000,000 t Musk estimates for a self-sufficient city.

windows.objective: First crew — arrives to a powered, provisioned, fueled base
enables: First crew lands at a base that is already powered, provisioned, and holds their return propellant.
Resident population: 12 — every per-capita clock (power, food area, habitat volume, imports) now has a denominator.
3,240 kWe of storm-proof power — roughly 2,700 homes' worth, with 60 kW of headroom over the installed load.
Batteries hold 19.1 MWh — 6 hours of full-base power with zero generation (dust storms are a non-event on fission).
Fuel plant produces ~6.3 t of methalox per day — a full Starship return load every 7 months.
8,701 t of propellant banked by the 2039-09 window — 6.2 Starship return flights' worth. The crew's ride home could be filled 6.2 times over.
2,700 m³ of pressurized volume beyond the ships — long-duration habitable space for ~108 people.
270 t of cached food/water/O2 — a crew of 12 could live on it for 148 months with zero recycling.
Robot workforce: 70 humanoids and 12 rovers.
EDL track record: landings so far demonstrate ~93% reliability (rule-of-three floor); this window's ships each land with ~86% odds.
Water independence: if mining stopped, ECLSS recycles ~90% and cached water covers the makeup for ~1,507 months (12 residents) — propellant production halts, the crew does not.
1,496 t of hardware on the surface — 3.6× the mass of the ISS, and 0.15% of the ~1,000,000 t Musk estimates for a self-sufficient city.

Agriculture: 30 modules can feed ~120 people (surplus vs 112 residents).
Recurring imports: residents need 260 t this synod at 10 t/person/yr (closure stage: none); manifests deliver 300 t.
7,080 kWe of storm-proof power — roughly 5,900 homes' worth, with 76 kW of headroom over the installed load.
Batteries hold 42.0 MWh — 6 hours of full-base power with zero generation (dust storms are a non-event on fission).
Fuel plant produces ~6.9 t of methalox per day — a full Starship return load every 7 months.
12,698 t of propellant banked by the 2041-11 window — 9.1 Starship return flights' worth. The crew's ride home could be filled 9.1 times over.
10,200 m³ of pressurized volume beyond the ships — long-duration habitable space for ~408 people.
570 t of cached food/water/O2 — a crew of 12 could live on it for 312 months with zero recycling.
Robot workforce: 130 humanoids and 20 rovers.
EDL track record: landings so far demonstrate ~96% reliability (rule-of-three floor); this window's ships each land with ~95% odds.
Water independence: if mining stopped, ECLSS recycles ~90% and cached water covers the makeup for ~341 months (112 residents) — propellant production halts, the crew does not.
3,459 t of hardware on the surface — 8.2× the mass of the ISS, and 0.35% of the ~1,000,000 t Musk estimates for a self-sufficient city.

rows.stmt: The first crew shall arrive at a powered, provisioned, and fueled Mars base with a demonstrated return path, and no single cargo-ship loss shall defeat the campaign.
rows.rationale: The program's definition of done for the pre-crew era.

rows.stmt: Starship shall reach orbit and be recovered.
rows.rationale: Nothing Mars-bound flies without orbital Starship; nearest gate.

rows.stmt: Both Starship stages shall be reused (routine refly).
Orbital propellant transfer (refill) shall be demonstrated.
rows.rationale: A Mars-bound ship launches nearly empty and refills in LEO; undemonstrated mid-2026.

rows.stmt: Long-duration cryogenic propellant storage (active chill) shall be demonstrated.
rows.rationale: Cryofuel must survive months in LEO and cruise; boil-off management unproven.

rows.stmt: The program shall demonstrate survivable EDL of a 100 t-class cargo ship on Mars.
rows.rationale: Heaviest object ever landed is ~1 t; 20-100x beyond demonstrated.

rows.stmt: At least 5 successful cargo landings shall be accumulated before crew commits.
rows.rationale: Statistical confidence in EDL beyond a single success.

rows.stmt: Follow-on ships shall land within meters of the base (precision landing aids operating).
The base shall have continuous, dust-storm-tolerant baseload power.
rows.rationale: Power is the tall pole; solar collapses in global storms.

rows.stmt: Installed generation shall reach 500 kWe or more in the first window.
rows.rationale: Pilot ISRU + margin; redundant fleet delivers ~640 kWe.

rows.stmt: Installed generation shall cover the delivered average load at every window.
rows.rationale: A power-starved plant silently derates ISRU production.

rows.stmt: Installed generation shall reach 1.5 MWe or more before crew arrival.
rows.rationale: Full-scale ISRU (~850 kW chain) plus habitat and margin.

rows.stmt: The program shall confirm and exploit accessible water ice (prospect, excavate, process).
The program shall produce and store a full crewed-return propellant load (1,400 t methalox) before crew commits.
rows.rationale: The single milestone that makes a crewed return credible; the deciding risk.

rows.stmt: An integrated pilot chain shall run at 40 kg/hr propellant-equivalent or more in the first window.
rows.rationale: Proves the full loop (capture-react-electrolyze-liquefy-store) at pilot scale.

rows.stmt: Chain nameplate rate shall reach one return load per synod (>= 112 kg/hr).
rows.rationale: The rate-matched fuel-factory buy (size_chain design point).

rows.stmt: Cumulative propellant produced and stored shall reach 1,400 t before crew departure.
At least two independent Sabatier chains shall exist on the surface before crew.
rows.rationale: Plant-level redundancy; a chain fault must not strand the crew.

rows.stmt: A near-closed life-support loop shall be demonstrated on Mars for 1,000 sols before crew.
rows.rationale: ISS-class ECLSS without spares gives <1% mission reliability; demo runtime is the proof.

rows.stmt: The ECLSS demonstration article shall be landed in the first window (demo clock).
rows.rationale: 1,000 sols of runtime requires delivery no later than 2 windows before crew.

rows.stmt: Pre-landed consumable caches of 90 t or more shall be on the surface before crew.
rows.rationale: Survival margin independent of ISRU and resupply.

rows.stmt: The life-support demonstration shall run as an integrated article: the ECLSS loop operating inside a landed, pressurized habitat, both on the surface for the full 1,000-sol clock.
rows.rationale: A closed loop can only be demonstrated against a sealed volume, and the integrated failure modes (interface leaks, condensation on the bladder wall, dust in heat exchangers) are the ones that matter. The habitat itself also needs long-duration demonstration.

rows.stmt: Pressurized habitat volume beyond the landed ships shall be ready before crew.
At least 5 habitat modules (rigid or inflatable) shall be on the surface before crew arrival.
A storm shelter and radiation protection shall be in place before crew.
A surface-to-Earth communications link shall operate from the first window.
The base shall operate autonomously under a 4-22 minute one-way light delay.
Surface mobility for cargo and instruments shall be operational from the first window.
Cargo offload, power distribution, and site logistics shall be operational from the first window.
Every flight window's manifest shall fit within its declared fleet's mass capacity.
Every flight window shall retain at least 10% fleet mass margin for growth and late additions.
rows.rationale: Concept-stage hardware grows; AIAA practice suggests far more than our 10% contingency. A window at 100% booked has no room for reality.

rows.stmt: No single cargo-ship loss in any window shall cost a schedule-critical capability (power, water, propellant chain, comms, autonomy, infrastructure, mobility, precision landing), accounting for hardware already on the surface.
rows.rationale: Crew-era capabilities may accept single-hull risk (re-flight margin); schedule-critical ones may not.

findings: Window 0 retires 50% of weighted program risk — the EDL, power, water, autonomy and comms unknowns die on first landing.
return_propellant_proven (weight 25) retires at 2033-03 — schedule-critical; a slip here slips first crew.
life_support_closed (weight 15) retires at 2035-05 — schedule-critical; a slip here slips first crew.
eclss_habitat: 1 unit(s) at 2031-01 counted as demonstration article(s) — early delivery here is deliberate risk buy-down, not idle mass.
habitat_inflatable: 1 unit(s) at 2031-01 counted as demonstration article(s) — early delivery here is deliberate risk buy-down, not idle mass.
No idle crew-era mass beyond verification margin — manifests are lean.
